prompt_text:
Analyze the following java source code repository for violations of the Dependency Inversion Principle (DIP).

## Principle Definition
High-level modules should not depend on low-level modules; both should depend on abstractions. Abstractions should not depend on details; details should depend on abstractions. When a class directly instantiates its dependencies instead of receiving them through injection, it violates DIP.

## What to Look For
- Classes that directly instantiate their dependencie...
raw_response:
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/ackedqueue/Queue.java",
      "entity_name": "Queue.openPages",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 172,
      "line_end": 222,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "High-level module depends on low-level implementation MmapPageIOV2",
      "reasoning": "The Queue class directly instantiates MmapPageIOV2, a concrete implementation for file I/O. This couples the high-level business logic of the queue to a specific storage implementation, violating the Dependency Inversion Principle."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/ackedqueue/Queue.java",
      "entity_name": "Queue.newCheckpointedHeadpage",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 298,
      "line_end": 305,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "High-level module depends on low-level implementation MmapPageIOV2",
      "reasoning": "The Queue class directly instantiates MmapPageIOV2. It should depend on an abstraction (e.g., PageIO interface) and receive an implementation factory or provider."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/common/io/DeadLetterQueueWriter.java",
      "entity_name": "DeadLetterQueueWriter.nextWriter",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 579,
      "line_end": 583,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "High-level module directly instantiates RecordIOWriter",
      "reasoning": "The DeadLetterQueueWriter directly instantiates the concrete class RecordIOWriter. This creates a hard-coded dependency on a low-level detail, preventing the use of alternative writers and complicating unit testing."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/plugins/factory/PluginFactoryExt.java",
      "entity_name": "PluginFactoryExt.plugin",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 169,
      "line_end": 218,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "High-level factory directly instantiates concrete plugin delegator classes",
      "reasoning": "The factory explicitly instantiates OutputDelegatorExt, filterDelegatorClass, and other concrete plugin implementations. It should depend on an abstraction or a registered provider rather than concrete classes."
    }
  ]
}

findings.description: High-level module depends on low-level implementation MmapPageIOV2
findings.reasoning: The Queue class directly instantiates MmapPageIOV2, a concrete implementation for file I/O. This couples the high-level business logic of the queue to a specific storage implementation, violating the Dependency Inversion Principle.

findings.description: High-level module depends on low-level implementation MmapPageIOV2
findings.reasoning: The Queue class directly instantiates MmapPageIOV2. It should depend on an abstraction (e.g., PageIO interface) and receive an implementation factory or provider.

The DeadLetterQueueWriter directly instantiates the concrete class RecordIOWriter. This creates a hard-coded dependency on a low-level detail, preventing the use of alternative writers and complicating unit testing.

findings.description: High-level factory directly instantiates concrete plugin delegator classes
findings.reasoning: The factory explicitly instantiates OutputDelegatorExt, filterDelegatorClass, and other concrete plugin implementations. It should depend on an abstraction or a registered provider rather than concrete classes.
